strengths: Strong SQL and complex query experience
Built ETL pipelines and data models
Experience with reverse ETL and integrations
weaknesses: No stated HIPAA or healthcare-regulated experience
Only ~2+ years in data roles vs required 3+
PHI access controls/compliance experience not shown
improvement_bullets: Developed robust data models using dbt to streamline transformations and maintain a consistent semantic layer, ensuring reliable metrics across all Power BI dashboards.
Implemented automated data quality testing and orchestration workflows using Python scripts to monitor pipeline health, preventing data inaccuracies before they reached executive reports.
Managed sensitive insurance data at Haven Technologies, strictly adhering to internal security protocols and data governance standards, providing a foundation for HIPAA-compliant handling of PHI.
Architected and maintained CI/CD pipelines for analytics code using Git, ensuring version control stability and enabling rapid, error-free deployment of new data models.
Built and optimized reverse ETL pipelines to sync modeled warehouse data back into Salesforce and production systems, closing the loop between analytics insights and operational action.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Analytics Engineer opportunity with Counsel Health. I am interested in this opportunity because I am deeply inspired by your mission to multiply healthcare capacity through AI-native tools, effectively bridging the gap between cutting-edge technology and patient-centered clinical care. What interests me about working for Counsel Health is your commitment to building a fully-vertical medical group; this ambitious approach to redesigning the clinical stack intrigues me because it creates a unique challenge for a data practitioner to build a semantic layer that truly defines the future of medical outcomes. I believe my expertise in architecting robust data pipelines and my ability to translate complex business needs into clean, scalable data models would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on the 'why' behind every data point. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in client onboarding and utilized Python and automation tools to overhaul our intake workflows. This resulted in saving 8 hours per week and allowed our team to refocus efforts on a marketing strategy that drove a 300% increase in customer acquisition. Ultimately, I know that the Analytics Engineer role at Counsel Health would allow me to bring my data modeling expertise to the core business metrics of your platform. Additionally, my role as a Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my skills in handling sensitive, regulated insurance data. By optimizing relational database queries and managing documentation, I reduced project delivery delays by 25% and ensured that our data structures were reliable enough for high-stakes insurance testing. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in both software engineering and analytics to build out your core data layer. I know that my proficiency in SQL and my experience in building mission-critical pipelines align with what it takes to succeed in the Analytics Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
